entry.title: Re: Public Cloud ))) No default value maintained for op....
entry.content:
Hi @DASUNGBAE 

 

Yes , the configuration activity your using is correct

The issue is outlined in the KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/0003250589

 

If the configuration is in display only then please check that the standard customizing role BPC_EXPERT 

is assigned and it has write restriction set as unrestricted in Maintain Business Role app

 

Best Regards

Enda

entry.title: Re: What’s New in SAP HANA Cloud – September 2024
entry.content: @Sven_Knöpfler - The private IP endpoint services offered by AWS, GCP , and Azure are proprietary to each individual hyperscaler. AWS supports PrivateLink connections within a region. Clients and servers do not need to be in the same data center or availability zone but they do have to be provisioned within the same AWS region.

entry.title: Re: MPN need to flow along with material number and MPN o...
entry.content:
Hi @gagan_14 

 

I'm not aware of any customizing in S/4HANA Cloud that can fit this requirement .

 

Please submit a feature request if you would liked it added in a future release by following the

2963059 ( https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/2963059 )

 

Best Regards

Enda

entry.title: Re: #COE#BUMA CDS views are not elegible as data source i...
entry.content:
Hi _JohnL_ 

The short answer looks to be "yes" however you should recognize there could be impacts.

The application is forcing you to change the recommendation to "No" meaning you understand that, while possible, the view is not recommended for that purpose.

Have a look in the View Browser app  and review the annotations section, especially: 

"OBJECTMODEL.USAGETYPE.DATACLASS
#TRANSACTIONAL
OBJECTMODEL.USAGETYPE.SERVICEQUALITY
#D
OBJECTMODEL.USAGETYPE.SIZECATEGORY
#XL
OBJECTMODEL.MODELINGPATTERN
#ANALYTICAL_CUBE"

Also review the help for the View Browser app as it has a lot of insights: 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/0bde695751505c08e10000000a441470.html?locale=en-US

That help link will also lead you to the Supported Capabilities (in that same tree is the Stability Contract that you should understand)

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/6a6ff32b25dd473080e6aeddbefecfca.html?locale=en-US

Finally, the community search can help you understand the various values, for example:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/cds-view-performance-annotation-for-cds-view-performance/ba-p/13440544

There are lots of reasons that SAP might not recommend the use of the particular view. The main reason is it could impact performance. 

Specifically, that view is designated as "XL" meaning you should expect a large amount of data returned. The Service Quality is "D" meaning Analytical reporting, and potentially >500ms to get the result. So you need to consider how often it is called, etc.

Yes you can use it, but it isn't recommended without understanding the limitations and you may want to put the appropriate filters in place. You may want to consider some alternate CDS Views to use in your External API use case.

I hope this helps

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: How Refresh POD after trigger a production Process
entry.content:
I've create a custom POD with a Button to AUTOCOLLECT a DATACOLLECTION Parameter from a PLC (ex Temperature) using a production process

 

When i Press the button the data is retrieved correctly but  the BAR in DATA COLLECTION LIST doesn't update.

To force the update i've to   switch between 2 operation activity going in the next one and returning to the original one connected to my data collection list.

 

Is there any way to refresh the datacollection list (bar green 1\1) in automatically as soon the data has been acquired ?

--Jerry

entry.title: Managing PFAS with SAP: Coping with Missing Product Compliance Data
entry.content:
How happy are you with your supply chain's responsiveness in providing product compliance information on PFAS, REACH, or RoHS?
Room for improvement? 😉

Don't worry, you are not alone. Hang in there and learn how to help yourself, leveraging your SAP system, to:   

  • Automate data collection from suppliers 
  • Develop a multichannel strategy, encompassing databases, tools, and networks  
  • Meet suppliers where they are. 

 

The Product Compliance World is Plagued by Data Gaps

This will not come as a surprise to you: Getting product compliance data from your supply chain is challenging.

The Volume of Data is Increasing Rapidly

More and more regulations are the main drivers for increasing data volume, with PFAS setting a new milestone for that. This goes along with heightened awareness by consumers and subsequently companies pushing their requirements down the supply chain.

The Landscape of the Supply Chain Data Exchange is Incredibly Heterogeneous

Stakeholders (consumers, customers, suppliers, authorities, governments)

  • Have different requirements & needs
  • Approach the challenges in different ways
  • Are hugely diverse

Let’s have a look at the last point for suppliers and their bandwidth of

  • size
  • industry
  • product compliance expertise and/or ambition
  • regional presence
  • willingness to answer or meet your requirements

This makes it impossible to pursue a one-size-fits-all solution. The reality looks like this:

  1. Suppliers with high bargaining power provide compliance information where and how they choose, not willing to fit to your scope, process or form.
  2. Customers with high bargaining power may request compliance information in a way that deviates from your usual practice.

Certain industries experience pockets of convergences. Other than that, it’s a wilderness out there.

The Product Compliance World Demands a Toolset of Solutions

Given the data gaps, a mono-solution won’t do it. You can use your bargaining power for parts of the market, but ultimately, you will be much more successful if you meet your suppliers where they are. Support them by making it as easy as possible for them. This means:

  • Accept different formats of product compliance data (e.g. certificate of compliance, IPC-175x standard, full material declarations, excel spreadsheets).
  • Implement a multichannel strategy. While contacting your suppliers by email, also use their or 3rd party websites to skim product information.
  • Automate as much as possible to avoid overwhelming your processes with the anticipated volume of data, e.g. related to PFAS.
  • Bundle requests and make it easy for suppliers to avoid supplier fatigue. Check if the information you are asking for can be applied to multiple regulations. Check if newly purchased components are covered by previous responses.

 

Transform Your Product Compliance World

Recognizing the increasing amount of time and resources our customers are spending to fill data gaps, we have developed opesus Product Compliance Collaboration (PCC).

If you run SAP S/4HANA for product compliance , SAP Product and REACH Compliance or SAP Product Safety : Jackpot! PCC is a centralized collaboration hub built on top of SAP Product Compliance applications

If you don’t run it: No worries! Read our previous article Managing PFAS with SAP: Unlock Your Data Treasures to learn about SAP Product Compliance applications.

 

PCC in a Nutshell

PCC complements your SAP system with:

  • A flexible design: PCC is designed to be adaptable to your needs. It is configurable for new reporting requirements.
  • Automated processing: PCC automatically initiates a data collection process based on incomplete compliance information in your SAP system. Responses are fed back to your SAP system.
  • A multichannel strategy: PCC interacts with suppliers, customers, authorities, and service providers. It triggers different collaboration modes like querying an external databases, networks or asking for direct input by a supplier based on your configuration.
  • Easy access: Externals can access PCC to provide direct input without running SAP.

A PCC data collection example using different strategies to close data gaps in your SAP system

 

The bottom line

The product compliance world is plagued by data gaps. Putting all your eggs in one basket won’t suffice. Even as an SAP customer running SAP Product Compliance applications, you must up your game with a sophisticated strategy.

What challenges have you encountered in managing PFAS regulations, and what solutions have you found effective?

What future developments or features would you like to see in SAP to better support PFAS management?

If you are curious about how PCC can help solve your compliance requirements, send me a message or leave a comment. I'm happy to demo our solution!

entry.title: Re: AI in SAP Ariba Invoicing - Intelligent Invoice Conversion
entry.content:
Thanks for the response Abhijit. I created the template and updated the email address in configuration.

When I am sending the pdf attachment to ARIBA test system it is throwing an exception that.
https://help.sap.com/docs/business-network-for-trading-partners/business-network-invoicing/uploading-pdf-and-scanned-invoices-using-email

 

An invoice that was sent via email for processing to the SAP Business Network team has failed during processing.

 
The invoice that was uploaded via email with the following details has failed processing :
 
Please send the invoice from an email address configured by your administrator in your SAP Business Network account.
 
 
Invoice Document969967462.PDF
 
Customer Name 
 
Received Time15 Oct 2024
 
Sincerely,
SAP Business Network team

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 3 Contest Activities
entry.content: It is working now, thanks @Dan_Wroblewski .

entry.title: How can I limit and specify the result of a foreach loop?
entry.content:
In order to personalize an email with ESL I'm trying to get a single result from a foreach loop, namely: the details of the next appointment in the future.

I'm using an if-statement wiothin the foreach loop to fetch only items that have a StartDate in the future:

<ul>
{% foreach item in rds.clinic.Agenda(contact.3200) limit 1 %}
	{% if item.StartDate | localized_date('nl','yMMdd') >= 'now' | localized_date('nl','yMMdd') %}
	<li>{{ item.Status }}</li>
	{% else %}
	<li>No items found.</li>
	{% endif %}
{% endforeach %}
</ul>

The ESL script renders all appointments, but only shows the Status of those in the future. However, if I try to limit the result it always renders the earliest items first.

Is there a way to only render the item whose StartDate is nearest in the future?

entry.title: Re: An error occurs while adding a new employee via Add N...
entry.content:
Hi @Razia ,

 

We are getting an error we got the logs the error is as below, could you please let us know if you have faced this error and how to resolve this:

- 'TimeSheetSummary' definition not found in MDF datamodel. Either the 'TimeSheetSummary' definition is invalid or Corresponding module is not enabled. 
- employeeTime of EmployeeTimeSheetEntry is not a GO 
- Null Go value found in part path timeType of path [employeeTime, timeType, externalCode]

entry.title: Embrace the Change: Champion the Composable Enterprise
entry.content:
Unsure about your existing systems and lacking visibility into your business processes?

Don't know which solution to choose?

Overwhelmed by countless pages of system setup?

Facing lengthy implementation projects that drag on for months?

In today's rapidly evolving world, agility is essential for business success. However, the fear of disrupting existing systems and the challenges of adopting new solutions can hold you back. So, what can you do?

When you consider it, what companies truly need are business processes or capabilities. They aren't concerned with the underlying systems or how to connect them. Imagine if you could purchase these readily available capabilities on the market, plug them in, and compose your own processes. After purchasing, a simple one-click installation would have your processes up and running in minutes instead of months, all while keeping technical complexities hidden. If a particular capability doesn't meet your needs, you can easily remove it. Plus, you'll only pay for what you use through a subscription-based model. Additionally, you'll gain greater clarity on your processes and discover new ideas for improvement.

Does this sound like a dream? This is the essence of a Composable Enterprise. If you're eager to learn more and turn this vision into reality, join us on this exciting journey with SAP architects as we explore this strategic topic and work towards making future enterprises more composable. Sign up using the link below: 

Composable Enterprise: Reimagining Enterprise Software for Unprecedented Business Agility 

Customer registration for the project is open until November 22. Once registered, you'll receive an invitation to an introductory call.

entry.title: Re: Login Failed after upgrade from FP18 to FP20 PL03
entry.content:
Hello,

Could it be that the affected CCOm user is identical with the integration user between CCO and CCOm (found under Configuration => Integration => SAP Customer Checkout manager => Webservice Endpoint)? And that CCO are still running on FP18?

Best regards,

Gunther

entry.title: SAP CPI - Receive http request with multipart/forrm-data on CPI
entry.content:
I try to send a http request from SAP ERP system to CPI with multiple files using Content-type multipart/form-data. On CPI i want to extract them as multiple attachments with a groovy script.

But I always end up with “No Attachments”. Therefore I suspect that the function message.getAttachments() is not correct.

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message

def Message processData(Message message) {
def attachments = message.getAttachments()

if (attachments == null || attachments.isEmpty()) {
messageLog.addAttachmentAsString("No Attachments", "No attachments found in the message.", "text/plain")
println "No Attachments"

return message
}

attachments.each { attachment ->
// message.setBody(attachment.getcontent())
}
return message
}

Test with client Postman

Appreciate your help.

 

Thank You

entry.title: Re: Login Failed after upgrade from FP18 to FP20 PL03

We uninstalled everything, removed the SAP folder where the manager has settings and reinstalled everything. We only used the same CCOm sql database and have the same issue. There is no any other running instances.

entry.title: Re: Login Failed after upgrade from FP18 to FP20 PL03

Hello Fedias,

 

Could you advise if some or all of the CCO POS stations are also upgraded to FP20 or are they still running on FP18? And what is their integration user from the CCO POS to CCO manager? Is it the same user that causes issues when logging on to CCO manager?

 

Kind regards,

Gunther

entry.title: Re: Service Center Different Catalogues
entry.content: Hi Nikos, Yes, you can definitely set up different catalogs in the SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Service Center (ECSC) based on the employee's country. Here are a few ways you can do this: Business Rules: You can set up rules that automatically show specific catalogs depending on an employee’s country. This way, they’ll only see content relevant to their location. Country-Specific Content Groups: ECSC allows you to create content groups that are tailored to different countries. These can be linked to the catalogs employees see, ensuring that the right info pops up for each region. Integration with SAP C4C: If you’re using C4C Service too, you can configure it to filter catalogs by location, so employees only see what’s relevant to them. Role-Based Permissions (RBP): Make sure permissions are set up so employees can only access catalogs meant for their country. This helps keep everything organized and secure. Hope this helps! Let me know if you need more details

entry.title: Re: Execution Only BOM usage is not allowed when plg and...

entry.title: How to Read User Details and User Groups Assigned in IAS for My CAP Application.
entry.content:
I have a CAP (Cloud Application Programming) backend built on Node.js and I’m using SAP UI5 for my frontend. I would like to retrieve the user groups that are assigned to the users in SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS) when they log in to my CAP application.

What are the best approaches to fetch the user groups and user details from IAS and pass that information to my CAP application? Could you guide me through the steps to achieve this?

entry.title: Re: [SAP BTP Onboarding Series] SAP BTP Cockpit - Global Account Technical Overview
entry.content:
Hi All ,

I have little confusion about creation and access of secondary administrator user for Global account / subaccount.

A global account administrator creates another administrator user for global account with email ID and SAP default  identity provider SAP service ID.  My question is:

1. Is the secondary administrator user  created automatically in the SAP default  identity provider SAP service ID during the creation in SAP BTP cockpit -> security->user?

2. when secondary administrator user is created, how to login to the BTP cockpit global account with secondary administrator user as there's no password assigned during the secondary administrator user creation. Is the secondary administrator user should already be registered with S-user in SAP for me?

When it comes to the BTP trial account, I have created administrator user for the subaccount, but how do I login to the subaccount with newly created administrator user (as there's no password)?

I hope my questions are not confusing.

All I want to know is when the second  administrator user is created for global account, how do I login with new created administrator user (as there's no password assigned during the creation)

Regards,

Satish

entry.title: Re: How to create a Catchup Bill in SAP Subscription Billing
entry.content:
Hi,

Subscription Billing splits bills by billing period. So, in your example you would get separate bills for the months September, October and November. You can configure subscriptions to be billed early so that they are billed 1 month plus 1 day before the start of a billing cycle. With such a setting, the month of November would be billed on September 30 as you described in your use case.

You can configure charges in the rate plan template such that no proration takes place in the first billing cycle. In your example, a subscription starting on Sept 12 would be billed for the entire month. A subscription starting after the 15th of a month would also be charged for the entire month. To prevent this, you can either work with waiver periods in the subscription item, or you let the subscription start on the 1st of the next month.

Kind regards,

Annette

entry.title: 2H2024 Learning - How does a Custom Card only show on a Custom Page?
entry.content:
Hi,

How do you set a custom card or banner to only show on a custom page and not show on the Learning Home page?

I can create a banner, a custom card, and a custom page. I cannot figure out how to have the custom card show on the custom page without having it also show on the learning home page. What am I missing?

Thank you for any assistance in how to do this,

Julie

entry.title: SAP Ariba Intake Management: A Seamless, User-Centric Solution with Powerful Extensibility
entry.content:
By Abdul Hakeem

In many large enterprises, procurement processes are often fragmented due to a heterogeneous and siloed solution landscape. End users typically face challenges navigating multiple systems and portals to manage various procurement needs, from simple purchase requests to onboarding new service providers. Employees often waste hours trying to figure out which system to use or how to track the status of their requests. This inefficiency not only frustrates users but also hinders professional oversight of company spending across all categories.

SAP Ariba Intake Management addresses these challenges by providing a fast, simple, and intuitive single front door for all procurement requests, streamlining processes and shielding users from system complexity. It simplifies the initiation, orchestration, and tracking of requests within complex software environments, eliminating friction and ensuring a smoother experience. This approach boosts user adoption and ensures compliant, well-governed spending without relying on enforcement.

Powered by Joule, SAP’s AI copilot, the solution offers a guided, context-aware experience with real-time visibility into request processing. It is also supported by SAP Signavio Process Intelligence for Process Mining for insights into efficiency and performance.

SAP Ariba Intake Management is built on SAP BTP, powered by SAP Build – a no-code platform that integrates seamlessly across SAP and non-SAP applications, enabling a unified user experience.

The solution’s modular composability provides standardized intake-to-procure and supplier management processes, with customizable building blocks and sub-processes. By leveraging SAP Build’s no-code tooling, process managers and super users can easily tailor workflows with drag-and-drop simplicity, utilizing an extensive API library and a rich selection of pre-built processes, workflows, and dashboard templates.

By simplifying procurement workflows and offering no-code customization, SAP Ariba Intake Management fosters innovation while ensuring compliant, efficient spending. This delivers an elevated user experience, frictionless compliance, and expanded control over enterprise spending with minimal effort.

entry.title: BW4 Hana and ILM
entry.content:
We use ILM in ERP.
Notifications arrive in BW4/Hana.

Unfortunately, no one from SAP can tell us how to model the data flow correctly.

We user:

  1. Activate Content 0BWTCT_DPP

ADSO                   0DPPNOT_C

ADSO                   0DPPNOT_I

ADSO                   0DPPNOT_R

  1. Transformation

Source: 0DPP_GEN_NOTIF to the ADSO 0DPPNOT_I

3 Transformation

Source:  ADSO 0DPPNOT_I  to   ADSO 0DPPNOT_R

Now the ADSO requires _R: Only the relevant notifications.

SAP says:
When transferring from 0DPPNOT_I to 0DPPNOT_R, it is checked whether a data flow is found in BW/4HANA for the ODP contained in the notification, as only then can associated replicated data be available. Please check whether there is at least one BW DataSource and one DTP for a data target (ADSO/IOBJ) for the ODPs (field 'DATASOURCE' in the DataSource or 'ODP_NAME' in the ADSO) from the notifications

What data flow is meant here ?
Unfortunately, there are no concrete instructions

Thanks for Help

Ralf

entry.title: PDF printing in ABAP - How can I ensure the correct format (A4/A3)?
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I am facing a problem when printing PDFs in ABAP. The PDFs are available as originals for documents and can be opened via transaction CV02N, for example.
Printing works in principle, I do this using ADS_SR_OPEN, ADS_SR_CONFIRM etc.

Now to my problem: Some PDFs are in A4 format, others in A3 format. When printing, however, everything is automatically set to A4, which means that content is cut off for larger formats.

How can I ensure that the PDFs are always printed in the correct format (A4 or A3)?
To make matters worse, I don't know which format a PDF is in before printing.

Many thanks in advance for your help!
Janik

entry.title: ""SETS" is not allowed here" - error message post conversion in S/4 HANA 2023, but no error in ECC.
entry.content:
Hello all,

We have a case where a custom table has a field 'SETS'. 

The code in ECC vs S/4 HANA is seen below:

There are no ABAP documentation on why it 'SETS' shouldn't be used. 

Any suggestions on why this is the case in S/4?
We can fix by below approach; any other suggestions are also welcome.

BR,

Ajay

entry.title: Re: TMS - Transport SAP Cloud Integration (CI/CPI) Content with Transport Management Service (TMS) a
entry.content:

We cannot select the entry in "Transport Nodes" button as shown below so unable to click on the import button as that is also disabled. We already checked the roles and we have the 'WorkspacePackagesTransport' and 'LandscapeOperator' roles assigned. So not sure what we are missing. Can you please help?

entry.title: Re: Unable to install ADT in Eclipse 2024-03 or 2024-06

entry.title: Re: Unable to install ADT in Eclipse 2024-03 or 2024-06
entry.content: After many struggles finally SAP released the fix... https://me.sap.com/notes/2369308/E (2369308 - Mirror/Download required Eclipse components for offline installation of the ABAP Development Tools) .

entry.title: Re: Unable to install ADT in Eclipse 2024-03 or 2024-06
entry.content: After many struggles finally SAP released the fix... 2369308 - Mirror/Download required Eclipse components for offline installation of the ABAP Development Tools) .

entry.title: Re: Unable to install ADT in Eclipse 2024-03 or 2024-06
entry.content: After many struggles finally SAP released the fix... 2369308 - Mirror2,Download required Eclipse components for offline installation of the ABAP Development Tools) .

Would you mind to elaborate it? 

Kind regards.

entry.title: Re: PDF printing in ABAP - How can I ensure the correct format (A4/A3)?
entry.content: What happens if you change the page format in the spool request with respect to A3/A4?

entry.title: Oportunidades de Ventas
entry.content:
Buenas tardes,

Necesito impedir que los usuarios eliminen las oportunidades de venta, excepto la gerente de ventas, intento hacerlo por transaction pero a la hora de intentar eliminar trae null el valor del OpprId.

Este es mi código:

IF :object_type = '97' AND :transaction_type = 'D' THEN

DECLARE usr nvarchar(10);
SELECT T0."U_USER" INTO usr FROM OOPR T0 WHERE T0."OpprId" = :list_of_cols_val_tab_del ;
IF :usr <> 21 THEN
error := 92;
error_message := 'TN-92: Este usuario no está autorizado para eliminar oportunidades de ventas.';
END IF

END IF

ese parametro :list_of_cols_val_tab_del no recupera el valor del formulario actual a la hora de dispararse la TN

Si alguien tiene este mismo caso, sería de mucha ayuda . Mi versión de SAP B1 es la 9.3 PL14

Saludos.

entry.title: Re: Unable to install ADT in Eclipse 2024-03 or 2024-06
entry.content: Thanks for the feedback. If the offline installation solved your question, you may post an answer and click on Accept solution. The note 2369308 is not a fix, it's a consulting note, here it's a how to... The solution was created in 2016. You have posted 4 comments, for information you may edit your comment, no need posting a new one at each revision.

entry.title: Multiple Positions with One Requisition
entry.content:
How do you post a requisition with multiple "seats" and fill each seat individually with a different position number. 

Example:  We need to hire 10 widget makers, all will be at the same location, shift etc so we are only posting one requisition with 10 positions. 

When filling the requisition with each new hire, how do you select individual position numbers when the requisition only has one position number listed.    Are you able to list multiple position numbers on the requisition so that each new hire can be onboarded into a different position number?

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP CDS Distinct does not return distinct values
entry.content:
Hi @Animatron56 ,

Could you try if the following works for you?

entity DistinctUsers as
    select
        ID,
        userId,
        userName,
        email
    from (
        select
            ID,
            userId,
            userName,
            email,
            row_number() over(
                partition by userId order by ID
            ) as rn
        from SAPUsers
    )
    where
        rn = 1;

Best,

Peter

entry.title: Re: Solution Process app dwonload best practices
entry.content:
Hi Rayane,

the first step would be to check if all the required data for the standard Sales processes can be accessed via the API. If the necessary data is available, the next step would be to configure the API. After that, a tool would need to be implemented to export the data into Excel.

Kind regards

Thomas

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano ,

I’m very interested in participating in the digital skills initiative. As a first-generation college student and a recent graduate with a Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science. I’m eager to kickstart my career in a dynamic and supportive environment.

I believe I’m a good fit for this program, as I’m currently looking to transition into a new career path. Financially, I face challenges in affording certification attempts on my own, so this initiative is particularly appealing to me. I’m motivated to enhance my skills and knowledge to succeed and grow professionally.

Thank you for considering my application, and I look forward to your response!

entry.title: Re: Generate SOAP services for your legacy RFCs to simplify integration out-of-the-box
entry.content:
Dear Martin,

what is the difference between your approach and the SAP standard functionality via SICF?
Just activate the following services in SICF:

/default_host/sap/bc/soap/rfc,
/default_host/sap/bc/soap/wsdl and
/default_host/sap/bc/soap/wsdl11.

Then you can get the WSDL file for every remote RFC enabled function module via e.g.
https://machine.name.com:port/sap/bc/soap/wsdl11?services=BAPI_SALESORDER_GETLIST&sap-client=010.

You get back a WSDL file that is ready to use.
Important: You must always add the client info in the URL, otherwise you get an HTTP 403.

Looking forward to your feedback!

Regards,
Andreas

entry.title: Re: Mask in CJ20N - "Screen" field
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for your help. However, the the project ID is blank because I erased it out in the image, since the mask ID represents confidential client data. The picture in the example is the "correct" picture, where the "screen" field is visible and populated... I would like to know how I can add this field in another client... iIs this a standard field or is it customed?

Thank you in advance

entry.title: CPU Core Count details and Ramp-up possibility
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I need your suggestion on the below queries as when we are choosing any RISE BASE/PREM edition the S4HANA Production application server RAM size is coming as 64GB and consists of 8-core CPU. Now my questions on the below -

1. As we can increase the RAM size to 128 - 256 till 512 GB for the application server. Will the CPU core increase along with ?

2. If yes, then is it by default provided by SAP when the server RAM size increase ? Or do we need to take into account for any additional Product ID along with the memory increase ?

3. Also if I want to only increase the CPU Core without increasing the RAM, will it be possible ?

RISE with SAP SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 

Best Regards,

Abhijit Sarkar
+91-8436500911

entry.title: HANA failed to start after updating to SLES 15 SP6 from SLES 15 SP5
entry.content:
HANA DB was working fine until I updated my SLES 15 from SP5 to SP6. This is on ppc64le

Error when doing HDB start:  process hdbdaemon HDB Daemon not running

When looking at the daemon logs, I see this:

[21152]{-1}[-1/-1] 2024-10-15 11:11:51.364509 i Daemon DaemonHandle.cpp(00340) : Process 'hdbnameserver', pid 21174 exited because it caught signal 11 with core
[21152]{-1}[-1/-1] 2024-10-15 11:11:51.364542 i Daemon DaemonHandle.cpp(00367) : Program 'hdbnameserver' failed during startup. Service is essential, shutting down all instances

I could not find much on the web when searching for this error. Is there any library that I need to manually install/update using zypper?

More info on system:

sh-4.4$ ./HDB version
HDB version info:
version: 2.00.059.04.1655794895
branch: fa/hana2sp05
machine config: linuxppc64le
git hash: 1cdbd35ec7472ab5d22a0bac02725a02dc3038f9
git merge time: 2022-06-21 09:01:35
weekstone: 0000.00.0
cloud edition: 0000.00.00
compile date: 2022-06-21 09:10:03
compile host: lsh35280le
compile type: rel

OS Info using: uname -a
Linux p10sles025 6.4.0-150600.23.25-default #1 SMP Tue Oct 1 10:54:01 UTC 2024 (ea7c56d) ppc64le ppc64le ppc64le GNU/Linux

Thanks in advance for any pointers!

Hi Ayman

entry.title: Re: Changes In Funds Management with S/4 Public Cloud
entry.content: That is correct. We have abolished the funds center and the commitment item as in OnPremise, we always suggested to use a 1:1 relationship between cost/funds center and commitment item/GL account. So now the structures have been streamlined in order to keep the 2 worlds in sync. We are not planning to change this in the cloud. Most feedback regarding this design has been very positive so far.

entry.title: In sap S4 Hana, not able to upload form template. Getting error form must not be changed in this sys
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I'm not able to upload form template after my changes in S4 Hana system. My form template starts with prefix 'ZF_*' instead of 'ZZ1_*'. I'm able to upload the form templates with ZZ1_* by successfully assigning it to package and transports using ‘Register Extensions for Transport’ app. But the form templates starting with 'ZF_*', I'm not able to change and not able to see in this app to reassign it to package. When I'm try to upload the form template getting error 'Form Template could not be updated' and Item ID 'ZF_*' must not be changed in this system. Could you please help me to figure out this issue.

 Thanks,

Mythili

entry.title: How to Implement Data Replication in SAP HANA Cloud for SAP CAP Microservices?
entry.content:
I’m currently working on an use case, an e-commerce application using SAP CAP (Java) in a microservices architecture. The application includes several services—product, price, stock, cart, order, user, and address—each of which has its own database in SAP HANA Cloud.

I’m exploring data replication strategies and would like to implement both master-master and master-slave replication across these services. Specifically, I’m seeking guidance on the following:

  1. How to configure master-master and master-slave replication in SAP HANA Cloud?
  2. What best practices or patterns should be followed to maintain data consistency and synchronization across services in a microservices architecture?
  3. Are there any SAP HANA Cloud-specific tools or configurations that would help with monitoring or optimizing the replication process?

Any insights, documentation, or examples would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

Abhinav

entry.title: SAP Certified Associate - Project Manager - SAP Activate
entry.content:
Hello
I see that there are 7 practice questions that are given with the learning journey. I am reaching out to see if there are any more additional practice question that I can take before I sit for the exam.

Do I have to go a Prometric or similar site to give the exam? Will appreciate if you can give me some details on how the exam is proctered.

entry.title: Choosing the Right Path for SAP S/4HANA Transformation: Greenfield, Brownfield, or Bluefield?
entry.content:
transformation involves several key steps

  1. System Conversion: Moving from the old SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA, which requires a technical upgrade and data migration.
  2. New Implementation: Implementing SAP S/4HANA from scratch for organizations starting fresh or restructuring their business processes.
  3. Selective Data Transition: Combining elements of both system conversion and new implementation, selecting specific data or processes to move.

The transformation offers improved performance, real-time analytics, and simplified IT landscapes due to S/4HANA’s in-memory database and modern user experience through Fiori.

 

Greenfield Implementation                  Brownfield Implementation                 Bluefield Implementation

A fresh, new implementation of SAP S/4HANA from scratch.A system conversion that migrates an existing SAP ERP system to S/4HANA.A hybrid approach that blends Greenfield and Brownfield strategies.
Used when starting with new processes or overhauling old systems.Keeps existing customizations, configurations, and processes while upgrading to S/4HANA.Selectively retains and transforms existing processes and data.
Ideal for companies looking for full transformation.Suitable for organizations aiming to keep most of their current system setup.Often used to minimize risk, time, and cost by combining the best of both worlds.

comparing the pros and cons of Greenfield, Brownfield, and Bluefield implementation in SAP:

Approach             Pros                                                                       Cons

Greenfield- Complete process redesign and optimization.
- Full flexibility in implementing best practices.
- No legacy system constraints.		- Time-consuming.
- Higher costs due to full rebuild.
- Requires significant change management.
Brownfield- Faster implementation.
- Retains existing customizations and processes.
- Less disruption to current operations.		- Limited flexibility to adopt new processes.
- Potential migration of inefficiencies.
- May require extensive technical adjustments.
Bluefield- Combines benefits of Greenfield and Brownfield.
- Selective process and data transformation.
- Reduces cost and risk of full rebuild.		- Can be complex to manage.
- Requires careful planning to balance old and new.
- May not achieve full innovation like Greenfield.

 

I tried to keep this article simple and high-level, making it easy for everyone from novices to experts.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi David,

I am Functional consultant and I identified as a person for this digital certification because I am a women and I am looking for a carrier change in SAP for carrier growth as SAP has high visibility in market. Also I am not able to afford the SAP certification cost. 

 

Regards

Pallavi

entry.title: Re: Companion for SAP Cloud Integration: Tools you love comes as a Docker Container
entry.content:
I know I'm a little late to the party, but it's really exciting to see this. I've recently begun to get more and more involved in CI work with my employer as we journey through a digital transformation. Having external tools to facilitate work and make the process smoother overall is very encouraging.

I've tried to access your website for the companion and some of the other tools you've so generously provided, and it looks like things aren't working correctly. I'd love to get more hands-on with these tools, but it seems like access is not open now.

Will it be opened up again soon?

Thank you in advance for any updates or assistance you can provide!!

entry.title: Re: Language based Email Triggering from IAS portal
entry.content:
Hi Sonia,

I think the problem from Michael is a problem with SF and  IPS/IAS. If you do not have EC Activated and you are using Api V2 in Target, the defautLocale form SF will send to IAS the language format "de_DE" and this will not work, since IAS need the language as "DE".

I have the same issue but we did not found any solution with SAP since all transformations are just for api V1.

We do not have any problems with LMS user only since LMS ist sending the language as DE.

Regards Cosmin

entry.title: Re: SAP Certified Associate - Project Manager - SAP Activate
entry.content:
Use this link to get many more practice questions: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/discovering-sap-activate-implementation-tools-and-methodology

Here is the link to know more on Activate: https://training.sap.com/certification/c_act_2403-sap-certified-associate--project-manager---sap-activate-g/
- From this link you can purchase exam vouchers

entry.title: Re: Mask in CJ20N - "Screen" field
entry.content: It is a standard field, it should be visible.

entry.title: Re: [CAP][CDS-TS] Powershell -> The term 'ts-node.exe' is not recognized as a name of a cmdlet,..
entry.content: Inside the cds-ts-ps1 file, I commented the assignment of $exe=".exe" (row 8 in my version) and then it works. I don't need any npx commands as an alternative..

entry.title: How to prevent users from submitting data within the MDF object on certain days
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I am trying to solve a business request:

We have a custom MDF on the people profile for benefits, and we want employees to inform us if they would like to opt for certain benefits (in this case, insurance). We need time to prepare the necessary paperwork, so we want our employees to provide us with their decision on the benefit by the end of October (with an effective date of January 1st of the next year). I am able to set up restrictions based on the effective date, but we need the system to check the date when the record was created in the system.

For example, I should be able to create a record with an effective date of 01.01.25 on 26.10.24 (since the creation date is before the end of October), but on 01.11.24, I should not be able to create a record (because the creation date is after the end of October).

Additionally, we have a requirement to set up a similar rule for each month (e.g., users should only be able to add records until the 25th of the month).

I found this blog: SAP Community Blog, but in this case, the effective date of the record is taken into consideration, which is not what I need.

Is this even possible?

have a great day,

Anna

entry.title: Re: ME21N Create Purchase Order with reference to previous Purchase Order
entry.content: resolvido como?

entry.title: Re: Mass Export of Performance Forms in SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content: We use the Archive and Print feature within Classical Reports pages, however they have a very low threshold of how many documents you can export/archive at a time its very difficult to come up with a strategy that allows you to get the full set of documents

entry.title: Re: 🟣 Guided Experiences with SAP Build Process Automation and Work Zone
entry.content: Good Session.

entry.title: automatic replenishment task exceeding maximum qty of bin
entry.content:
Hello SAP World,

we are replenishing at bin level using automatic replenishment. We have activated replenishment for storage type. we have maintained fixed bin assignment with min and max qty at bin level.

Testing: once pick task is created and confirmed. if stock level is less than min qty,  replenishment task is getting created automatically.  But the task is getting created for full max qty. it is not calculating qty to meet the max qty.

Eg: min qty:5 pc , max qty:10 pc

stock available:2 pc

replenishment task created: 10 pc.

But it should have been created for 8 pc.

Please recommend solution.

entry.title: RBP without employee central?
entry.content:
We are implementing Recruiting module in SuccessFactors. We don't have any employee central. My question is how I am going to assign the roles and authorizations to user without EC? The target groups can be managers or regional heads? I would appreciate any quick response. 

Thanks 

#RBP #RoleBasePermissions #Recruiting

Regards,

Nirmala

entry.title: Upon attempting RMA PGI receive the following error "Delv qty must be entered for the item."
entry.content:
I am trying to PGI a ZKA Consignment for Pick up RMA but I receive the message below. "Delivery qty must be entered for the item - VL361
Full Delivery is required VL150

What step am I missing?

entry.title: Strange RAP runtime behavior during validation on save
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am noticing a very strange behavior in my RAP application (Managed)

As per SAP help documentation - AUTHORITY-CHECK statement cannot be used in VALIDATION ON SAVE.

But I used AUTHORITY-CHECK statement in handler method of VALIDATION ON SAVE to validate user authorization, and not getting any error/behavior illegal statement exception.

Note:- There is not any authorization context is defined to disable authorization.

Do you know the reason, or is SAP help documentation not updated correctly?

entry.title: Re: How to convert Text to numeric format in Webi when I...
entry.content:
Define the following variable: 

v_Date:     =FormatDate(ToDate([Date]; "MMMyyyy dd"); "MMyyyy dd")

 (Note: [Date] object in my example = [OCALMONTH] object)

... 

I hope it helps

entry.title: SAP CAP Multi-tenant scenario with central app router and multiple services
entry.content:
Hi community,
I am working in a multi-tenant environment scenario where I have two multi-tenant services, and I want to create only one app-router that will be responsible to handle the incoming requests for both services. (I have already done this in a single tenant scenario).
I created my central app-router and the mta.yaml look like this:

 

_schema-version: "3.1"
ID: poc-launchpad
version: 1.0.0
description: "A simple CAP project."
parameters:
  enable-parallel-deployments: true
modules:
 # --------------------- APPROUTER MODULE ---------------------
  - name: poc-launchpad
    type: approuter.nodejs
    path: .
    parameters:           
      disk-quota: 512M
      memory: 512M
      keep-existing-routes: true      
    requires:
      - name: poc-launchpad-approuter-auth
      # - name: poc-mt-customers-auth
      - name: poc-launchpad-destination
      - name: poc-launchpad-approuter-html5-runtime
      - name: poc-launchpad-logging
    properties:
      TENANT_HOST_PATTERN: "^(.*)-XXX-dev-dev-poc-launchpad.cfapps.us10.hana.ondemand.com"      
resources:
    # ----------------- DESTINATION --------------------------------------          
  - name: poc-launchpad-destination
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------   
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service    
    parameters:
      # tenant-mode: dedicated
      service: destination
      service-plan: lite
      config:
        HTML5Runtime_enabled: true
        init_data:
          instance:
            existing_destinations_policy: update
            destinations:
              - Authentication: NoAuthentication
                Name: ui5
                ProxyType: Internet
                Type: HTTP
                URL: https://ui5.sap.com           
  # ---------------- APPLICATION LOGGING SERVICE ---------------
  - name: poc-launchpad-logging
    # ------------------------------------------------------------
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      service: application-logs
      service-name: poc-launchpad-logging-${space}
      service-plan: lite
  # ---------------- AUTHORIZATION SERVICE ---------------
  - name: poc-launchpad-approuter-auth
    # ------------------------------------------------------------
    parameters:
      path: ./xs-security.json
      service-plan: application
      service: xsuaa
      config:
        xsappname: poc-launchpad-${org}-${space}
        tenant-mode: shared
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service          
    
  # - name: poc-mt-customers-auth
  #   type: org.cloudfoundry.existing-service 

  # ---------------- HTML5 RUNTIME ---------------  
  - name: poc-launchpad-approuter-html5-runtime
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      service-name: poc-launchpad-approuter-html5-runtime
      service-plan: app-runtime
      service: html5-apps-repo

 

But unfortunately, when I enter to my app router it is not properly authenticating to my destination (the destination is created in the tenant sub-account). 

If I change my authorization service  poc-launchpad-approuter-auth that is not working, for the authorization service that I use for my application:

 - name: poc-mt-customers-auth

 type: org.cloudfoundry.existing-service

it is able to access my application as expected. I think my problem is with the authorization service, I have read some documentation and I have found multi-tenant examples but all of them are with one mta.yaml that has the app-router, the services, and the web content in one file one application, but that is not my case.

any suggestion or example that address this scenario will be appreciated.

Thanks,

Tatiana F.
